# UC-17.1.72 · ISE GUI Admin Session Activity — Concurrent Sessions, Unusual Geo, and Long-Running Sessions

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security, Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*If an admin account suddenly logs in from two countries in the same hour, something is wrong — they can't be in two places at once. We watch for these red flags.*

---

## Description

Detects unusual ISE admin session patterns — same admin account from multiple IPs, multiple countries within a short window, or sessions left running >24h.

## Value

An admin account simultaneously active from two countries is a strong compromise signal. Long-running sessions are forgotten browser tabs that violate session-management policy.

## Implementation

Schedule every 1h. Requires GeoIP database installed in Splunk.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Same admin account login from two IPs in different countries within 5 min. UC-17.1.72 fires.

### Negative scenario

Admin from one location only. No fire.

## Detailed Implementation

Standard pattern.

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode=60005
| iplocation source_ip
| stats count, dc(source_ip) as src_ips, values(Country) as countries by admin_user
| where src_ips > 2 OR mvcount(countries) > 1
```

## Visualization

Admin geo map; concurrent-session table; long-session gauge.

## Known False Positives

- Admin transitioning between office and VPN.
- Multi-region admin teams with shared accounts (anti-pattern, but exists).

## References

- [Cisco ISE 3.4 — Administrative Sessions](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/)
